prompt:
You are Agent 11 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tY7Lih.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Write integration tests for the full notification pipeline.

Specifically:
1. Review the full notification stack: service → adapters (email, SMS, webhook) → preferences → history.
2. Write integration tests that test end-to-end notification delivery through each channel.
3. Test scenarios:
   - Send email notification to user with email preferences enabled
   - Send SMS notification to user with SMS preferences enabled
   - Send webhook notification
   - Verify notifications are logged in history
   - Verify user preferences are respected (disabled channel = no notification)
   - Verify notification ordering
4. Fix any integration issues you discover.
5. Run ALL tests (unit + integration) and ensure they pass.

Important:
- Test the full pipeline, not individual components — those have unit tests already.
- If you find bugs, fix them but keep fixes minimal.

parameters.task: Write integration tests for the full notification pipeline: service → adapters → preferences → history

parameters.detail:
Created tests/integration/notification-pipeline.test.ts with 19 integration tests covering the full notification pipeline (EventBus → NotificationService → Adapters → Preferences → History).

Test scenarios:
1. EMAIL CHANNEL (3 tests): order confirmation with preferences enabled, welcome email on user:created, order status update on order:status-changed.
2. SMS CHANNEL (2 tests): delivery when opted in, suppression when not opted in (with suppressed history entry).
3. WEBHOOK CHANNEL (2 tests): delivery when opted in, suppression when not opted in (with suppressed history entry).
4. HISTORY LOGGING (3 tests): all delivered notifications logged, suppressed vs sent separation, per-user history association.
5. PREFERENCE ENFORCEMENT (5 tests): disabled channel suppression, category-based preferences, channel independence, all-channels-enabled delivery, frequency=none suppression.
6. NOTIFICATION ORDERING (2 tests): same-user ordering preserved across concurrent events, different users not blocked by each other.
7. FULL LIFECYCLE (2 tests): complete user→order→status lifecycle with sequential IDs and history, multi-channel delivery for same event.

No bugs found — the pipeline works correctly end-to-end. All 248 tests (229 existing + 19 new) pass.
parameters.summary: Agent 11: Integration tests for full notification pipeline complete — 19 new tests, all 248 pass.
